title: aster incanus
intro: Aster incanus, also known as smooth aster, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that grows in North America, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. The medicinal properties of Aster incanus lie in its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to be due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Aster incanus include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Aster incanus can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
